page: Amazon Web Services c8a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 processors built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 192 physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The memory configuration consists of 384.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM operating at 6 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core. This instance relies on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data places this instance in the top 10% tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 33,045.2 GB/sec and uncached large block writes at 383.09 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and cryptographic acceleration. These capabilities translate to top-tier performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations.

The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. For large language model workloads, it achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds, though text generation performance ranges from average to strong depending on model size. Given the high compute density, lack of local storage, and 1:2 memory-to-core ratio, the c8a.48xlarge is optimized for compute-heavy workloads such as high-performance computing, batch processing, distributed analytics, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9R45 processors running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmark results place the c8a.48xlarge in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations. It supports AVX-512 and AVX_VNNI instructions, making it highly effective for compute-intensive tasks. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads such as software compilation, high-performance computing, distributed data processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.48xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-core-density, compute-optimized server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier CPU, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic benchmark performance
Optimized for high-performance computing, software compilation, and distributed analytics
